findings.evidence: `Basic.lean:99` has `operator : ℝ → X →L[ℝ] X`; `Basic.lean:103-107` guard the laws/continuity by nonnegative time, and `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/OneParameterSemigroups/README.md:37` reserves two-sided `t∈ℝ` time for C₀ groups rather than semigroups.
findings.fix: Index the semigroup by `ℝ≥0` (or otherwise quotient/extensionalize the structure by agreement on `Set.Ici 0`) and coerce to `ℝ` only in analytic estimates/integrals.
findings.issue: `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.operator : ℝ → X →L[ℝ] X` includes arbitrary negative-time operators, while `semigroup`, `strong_cont`, `contracting`, `HasGrowthBound`, the generator, and the resolvent only use `t ≥ 0`; two identical C₀-semigroups can be unequal because of irrelevant negative-time choices.

summary: The core results are stated at the intended real-Banach and general growth-bound level, but the main semigroup structure stores strictly more time data than a C₀-semigroup has. That makes the API non-extensional on the mathematical object it is meant to model.
